purpose.summary: This copybook defines the data structures COPAU1AI and COPAU1AO, which are used for screen input and output, potentially related to authorization processing. COPAU1AI defines the input fields with associated length and flag fields, while COPAU1AO redefines COPAU1AI to provide a different view of the same data, using single-character fields for cursor positioning and other attributes.

paragraphs.purpose: This data structure defines the input fields for a screen, likely related to authorization processing. It contains various fields such as TRNNAMEL/I/F/A (Transaction Name), TITLE01L/I/F/A (Title 1), CURDATEL/I/F/A (Current Date), PGMNAMEL/I/F/A (Program Name), TITLE02L/I/F/A (Title 2), CURTIMEL/I/F/A (Current Time), CARDNUML/I/F/A (Card Number), AUTHDTL/I/F/A (Authorization Date), AUTHTML/I/F/A (Authorization Time), AUTHRSPL/I/F/A (Authorization Response Code), AUTHRSNL/I/F/A (Authorization Response Name), AUTHCDL/I/F/A (Authorization Code), AUTHAMTL/I/F/A (Authorization Amount), POSEMDL/I/F/A (Point of Service Entry Mode), AUTHSRCL/I/F/A (Authorization Source), MCCCDL/I/F/A (Merchant Category Code), CRDEXPL/I/F/A (Card Expiration Date), AUTHTYPL/I/F/A (Authorization Type), TRNIDL/I/F/A (Transaction ID), AUTHMTCL/I/F/A (Authorization Match Code), AUTHFRDL/I/F/A (Authorization Fraud Date), MERNAMEL/I/F/A (Merchant Name), MERIDL/I/F/A (Merchant ID), MERCITYL/I/F/A (Merchant City), MERSTL/I/F/A (Merchant State), MERZIPL/I/F/A (Merchant Zip), and ERRMSGL/I/F/A (Error Message). Each field is defined with its length (L), input/output flag (F), attribute character (A), and input field (I). The structure appears to be designed for capturing and displaying authorization-related data on a screen.
paragraphs.dead_code_reason: Record layout 'COPAU1AI' is never used by any program

paragraphs.purpose: This data structure redefines COPAU1AI to provide a different perspective on the same data, primarily for screen output formatting and control. It uses single-character fields (C, P, H, V, O) to represent cursor positioning, highlighting, and other display attributes for each corresponding field in COPAU1AI. For example, TRNNAMEC/P/H/V/O correspond to TRNNAMEL/I/F/A in COPAU1AI, allowing the program to control the display attributes of the Transaction Name field. The 'C' suffix likely represents the cursor position, 'P' the protection attribute, 'H' the highlight attribute, 'V' the validation attribute, and 'O' the output field. This redefinition enables precise control over the screen's appearance and user interaction, facilitating a user-friendly interface for authorization-related transactions.
